title: Waystation V1 implementation rules
purpose: Keep Waystation's first implementation small, dogfoodable, and faithful to the local-first ledger model.
instructions: Build Waystation as a Bun + TypeScript CLI first. Canonical state is JSON files under .waystation/ (config.json, per-record *.json, append-only events.jsonl). Treat SQLite (bun:sqlite) as a rebuildable index, never the source of truth. Generate Markdown one-way from JSON for humans; never parse it back. Defer the dashboard, GitHub sync, worktree automation, and agent launching until the core CLI and brief workflow are useful.
must_do: Validate every record through zod on read; serialize from validated objects on write.
Route all mutations through the single core write path (lock + atomic write + event append).
Keep commands usable by coding agents (stable output, --json on reads).
Keep implementation slices small enough to review (walking skeleton, then widen).
Check your inbox (`waystation inbox --agent {{agent}}`) when you start, and periodically while working — other agents may have posted questions, updates, or verdicts.

Post a message to the task thread (`waystation message post --thread {{task_id}} --from {{agent}}`) at meaningful checkpoints: notable progress, a decision, a blocker, or a question for another agent.
Answer questions addressed to you (kind=question) before they block another agent; reply with in_reply_to to keep the thread coherent.
must_not: Make SQLite the canonical source of truth.
Require a daemon for V1 CLI use.
Require GitHub, Claude Code, or any specific agent.
Build the dashboard before the CLI model settles.
Hand-edit canonical JSON as the normal write path (use the CLI/MCP).
Go silent on a long task — parallel agents and auditors rely on your posted updates to stay in sync.

finish: waystation message post --thread {{task_id}} --from {{agent}} --kind update --body "<summary of what changed>"
